hero.title: Borderlands Mobile
hero.description: A fast-entry Borderlands hub focused on iPhone launch access, release updates, mission modes, loot basics, and future Android rollout tracking.

gameFeature.title: Borderlands Mobile Gameplay
gameFeature.description: Jump into a cel-shaded looter shooter on iPhone. Run Campaign missions, level up your Vault Hunter, collect weapons across five rarity tiers, and push your Power Score higher as you conquer Pandora.

tools.title: Borderlands Mobile
tools.subtitle: Essential guides for Vault Hunters — download, beginner tips, class overview, controls, leveling, power score, skills, gear upgrades, mods, campaign, Vaughn's Adventure, Marcus Munitions, Tower of Terror, Circle of Slaughter, ship upgrades, and Battle Pass

borderlandsMobileDownloadAndDeviceRequir.title: Download Borderlands Mobile and Check iPhone Requirements
borderlandsMobileDownloadAndDeviceRequir.subtitle: Fastest scan block for new players: platform, device specs, install size, and current status at a glance.
borderlandsMobileDownloadAndDeviceRequir.intro: Borderlands Mobile is available on the App Store as a free iPhone release from Zynga. The game is currently in a limited-time test, recommends playing on iPhone 11 or newer, and is not optimized for tablets or iPads.

items.label: Tablet support
items.value: Not optimized for tablets or iPads

borderlandsMobileBeginnerGuide.title: Your First Hours in Borderlands Mobile
borderlandsMobileBeginnerGuide.subtitle: Organize the early-game loop into clear actions — Campaign, XP, and Power Score checks that matter most when missions get harder.
borderlandsMobileBeginnerGuide.intro: The cleanest early-game route is to enter Campaign, push Main Missions first, collect Side Missions as they unlock, and keep your level and gear moving together. Watch your Power Score instead of pushing blindly.

steps.title: Open Travel and enter Campaign
steps.description: Tap the Travel button, then choose Campaign to begin your journey through Pandora.

steps.title: Push Main Missions first
steps.description: Main Missions drive the chapter storyline and are the clearest way to move progression forward.

steps.title: Pick up Side Missions when they appear
steps.description: Side Missions unlock as you progress through chapters and offer optional objectives with extra rewards.

steps.title: Farm XP in Campaign mode
steps.description: Your Vault Hunter gains XP by completing missions and defeating enemies. You can level up in real time while still in the field.

steps.title: Use level-ups to stay durable
steps.description: Each higher level permanently increases your Vault Hunter's Power Score and Max HP, and unlocks better rewards from Vaughn's Adventure.

steps.title: Check Power Score before hard missions
steps.description: Power Score rises as you level up and equip stronger gear. Missions show a recommended minimum Power Score — staying close makes progression smoother.

steps.title: Replay cleared stages for Star Challenges
steps.description: After completing several chapters, Star Challenge unlocks on finished missions and adds up to three extra challenges per mission for more rewards.

quickTips: Campaign is also the primary XP source — stay in it for the first few hours.
Power Score is the most important early progression number to watch.
Side Missions are optional but give extra gear and XP.
Star Challenges extend replay value once you have cleared the main stages.

borderlandsMobileSummonerClassGuide.title: How the Borderlands Mobile Summoner Class Works
borderlandsMobileSummonerClassGuide.subtitle: Understand what the Summoner does, its Action Skill, and how the skill system opens up from level 8.
borderlandsMobileSummonerClassGuide.intro: Summoner is the only available class in the current Borderlands Mobile build. The class centers on phase-energy beast summoning and opens a full skill system at level 8 with room for future class expansions.

items.title: Only available class
items.body: Summoner is the sole playable class in the current Borderlands Mobile build.

items.title: Class identity
items.body: The Summoner wields phase energy to summon beasts that fight alongside you in battle.

items.title: Action Skill: Good Boy!
items.body: Summons Nigel — a companion beast — for a limited time to fight alongside you.

items.title: Skills unlock at level 8
items.body: The full skill tree becomes available at level 8, giving access to stat upgrades and ability unlocks.

items.title: How Skill Points work
items.body: Every level-up grants one Skill Point. Spend points to increase stats and unlock or upgrade skills tied to your equipped class.

items.title: Loadout flexibility
items.body: Unlocked Shared skills from different classes can be mixed into your active loadout.

items.title: Future expansion
items.body: Additional classes and customization options are planned for upcoming Borderlands Mobile updates.

borderlandsMobileControlsGuide.title: Learn the Borderlands Mobile Touch Controls Fast
borderlandsMobileControlsGuide.subtitle: Turn the mobile HUD into a clear first-session walkthrough — movement, aiming, firing, and quick fixes for smoother combat on iPhone.
borderlandsMobileControlsGuide.intro: Borderlands Mobile uses a dual-side touch layout: movement on the left, camera control on the right. Once you understand ADS, autofire behavior, and reload timing, the combat loop becomes much less clumsy on phone.

steps.title: Move on the left, look on the right
steps.description: Drag on the left side of the screen to move, and drag on the right side to pan your view.

steps.title: Use jump aggressively — double jump available
steps.description: Tap the jump button on the right side of the screen. Double jump is supported.

steps.title: Use ADS for focused aiming
steps.description: Tap and hold the Aim Down Sight button, then drag to adjust your aim for better accuracy.

steps.title: Let aiming trigger autofire
steps.description: When your reticle is on enemies or targets, the game can shoot automatically while you hold aim.

steps.title: Switch to manual fire when needed
steps.description: Tap the fire button to shoot manually. Autofire can be toggled on or off in Settings.

steps.title: Reload manually between pushes
steps.description: Use the reload button to reload by hand. Your Vault Hunter also reloads automatically when the magazine empties.

steps.title: Quick iOS cleanup if controls feel bad
steps.description: Check your connection, update from the App Store, and restart the app or device when you run into loading or crashing problems.

quickTips: ADS significantly improves accuracy — use it whenever you need precision shots.
Autofire can be turned off in Settings for players who prefer manual trigger control.
Double jump gives strong vertical advantage in close-quarters encounters.
Reload manually before entering high-density areas to avoid mid-fight delays.

borderlandsMobileLevelingGuide.title: How Leveling Works in Borderlands Mobile
borderlandsMobileLevelingGuide.subtitle: Leveling is the core early progression loop. It raises combat stats, improves side-feature rewards, and feeds directly into skill growth.
borderlandsMobileLevelingGuide.intro: Your Vault Hunter gains XP in Campaign mode by completing missions and killing enemies. Levels can increase in real time during a mission, and every new level permanently boosts Power Score and Max HP while also improving the rewards from Vaughn's Adventure.

items.title: Earn XP in Campaign mode
items.description: XP comes from two main sources: completing missions and defeating enemies in Campaign mode.

items.title: Track the yellow XP ring
items.description: The yellow progress line around your Vault Hunter level shows current XP progress. When the line completes the circle, you level up.

items.title: Level up during missions
items.description: You do not need to finish a mission before gaining a level. Your Vault Hunter can level up on-field in real time.

items.title: Get permanent stat growth
items.description: Each higher level gives a permanent increase to Power Score and Max HP.

items.title: Boost Vaughn's Adventure rewards
items.description: After rescuing Vaughn, Vaughn's Adventure unlocks. It grants extra XP and Cash on a 10-minute timer and a Legendary Chest on an 18-hour timer. Reward amounts increase as your level rises.

items.title: Turn levels into skills
items.description: Skills unlock at level 8, and every level-up grants 1 Skill Point for unlocking or upgrading skills.

borderlandsMobilePowerScoreGuide.title: Power Score Explained in Borderlands Mobile
borderlandsMobilePowerScoreGuide.subtitle: Power Score measures how battle-ready your Vault Hunter is. It grows through leveling and better gear, and missions use it as a readiness benchmark.
borderlandsMobilePowerScoreGuide.intro: The game uses Power Score as a simple snapshot of combat strength. Your total goes up when your Vault Hunter levels up and when you equip stronger gear, while each individual piece of gear contributes based on its own level and rarity.

items.details: It is the main indicator of how strong your current Vault Hunter setup is.

items.value: Level up and equip stronger gear
items.details: Your total Power Score keeps increasing as your Vault Hunter levels up and your loadout improves.

items.value: Each gear piece has its own Power Score
items.details: A gear item's score depends on its level and rarity.

items.value: Missions show a recommended minimum Power Score
items.details: You can still enter below the recommendation, but the mission becomes much harder to clear.

items.value: Higher rarity usually means higher Power Score
items.details: Gear rarities are Common, Uncommon, Rare, Epic, and Legendary, but item level still matters.

items.value: Power Score Track unlocks claimable rewards
items.details: Claim milestone rewards from the profile avatar menu in the top-left corner of the HQ screen.

borderlandsMobileSkillsGuide.title: Borderlands Mobile Skills, Skill Points, and Early Build Planning
borderlandsMobileSkillsGuide.subtitle: Skills open the door to build customization. Hit level 8, earn points through leveling, and spend them carefully across unlocks and upgrades.
borderlandsMobileSkillsGuide.intro: Skills become available once your Vault Hunter reaches level 8. From there, every level grants a Skill Point, and you can use those points to raise stats, unlock new abilities, and customize loadouts with Shared skills.

items.title: Unlock Requirement
items.description: Skills unlock when your Vault Hunter reaches level 8.
bullets: No skills are available before level 8
Skill access is tied directly to character progression

items.title: How Skill Points Work
items.description: Each time your Vault Hunter levels up, you earn 1 Skill Point.
bullets: Level-ups continuously feed your skill progression
Skill Points are used for both unlocks and upgrades

items.title: What Skills Do
items.description: Skill Points can increase stats and unlock new skills for your equipped class.
bullets: You can inspect a skill before spending points
Advanced Skills require more Skill Points

items.title: Progression Rule
items.description: More skills become available after you spend a certain amount of Skill Points.
bullets: Early choices open up later parts of the tree
Point spending order shapes your build path

items.title: Shared Skills
items.description: Unlocked Shared skills can be mixed and matched from different classes to personalize loadouts.
bullets: Shared skills support flexible loadout building
Customization expands as more classes and skills are unlocked

items.title: Current Live Class
items.description: In the current version, the available class is Summoner.
bullets: Summoner uses phase energy to summon beasts
Its Action Skill is "Good Boy!", which summons Nigel to fight alongside you

borderlandsMobileGearUpgradeGuide.title: Borderlands Mobile: How to Upgrade Weapons, Grenades, and Shields
borderlandsMobileGearUpgradeGuide.subtitle: Rank upgrades are a direct way to raise Power Score. Each gear category uses its own material type plus Coins, so efficient spending matters early on.
borderlandsMobileGearUpgradeGuide.intro: You can upgrade the Rank of your Grenade, Weapons, and Shield from the Inventory screen. Every rank increase raises your Vault Hunter's Power Score, and each gear category uses a specific upgrade material from missions, rewards, and scrapping matching gear.

items.title: Open Inventory
items.description: Go to your Inventory and select the Upgrade option for the gear type you want to improve.

items.title: Upgrade Grenades
items.description: Grenade rank upgrades require Fillers and Coins. Fillers come from Missions, Rewards, and scrapping Grenades, while Coins come from mission gameplay and completion rewards.

items.title: Upgrade Weapons
items.description: Weapon rank upgrades require Components and Coins. Components come from Missions, Rewards, and scrapping Gear.

items.title: Upgrade Shields
items.description: Shield rank upgrades require Cells and Coins. Cells come from Missions, Rewards, and scrapping Shields.

items.title: Prioritize for Power Score
items.description: Rank upgrades directly increase Power Score. Upgrade pieces that strengthen your current mission readiness the most.

items.title: Compare rarity and level together
items.description: Higher rarity gear usually has a higher Power Score, but a higher-level lower-rarity item can still be the better short-term choice.

borderlandsMobileModsGuide.title: Borderlands Mobile Mods Guide
borderlandsMobileModsGuide.subtitle: Mods are items you attach to weapons to improve their performance. Equip and swap them from the Inventory at any time, and farm more through Circle of Slaughter.
borderlandsMobileModsGuide.intro: Use this page to understand what Mods do, where to equip them, and which gameplay loops support mod-focused loadout progression in Borderlands Mobile.

items.title: What Mods Do
items.text: Mods are items you can attach to a weapon to improve its performance, with many kinds of Mods offering different stat bonuses.

items.title: Where to Equip Mods
items.text: Open your Inventory and tap the Mods tab on the upper-right side of the screen to manage all your equipped Mods.

items.title: Swap Mods Anytime
items.text: Mods can be equipped and unequipped within the Inventory at any time without any cooldown or restriction.

items.title: Mod Upgrade System
items.text: The Borderlands Mobile Mod Upgrades section is listed as Coming Soon in the current build, with an expanded system planned for future updates.

items.title: Farm Mods via Circle of Slaughter
items.text: Circle of Slaughter has no entrance fee, currently offers two levels, and gives higher chances to win Gear and Mods the longer you survive enemy waves.

items.title: Rank Materials Quick Reference
items.text: Weapon rank upgrades use Components and Coins, Grenade rank upgrades use Fillers and Coins, and Shield rank upgrades use Cells and Coins.

borderlandsMobileCampaignGuide.title: Borderlands Mobile Campaign Guide
borderlandsMobileCampaignGuide.subtitle: Campaign is the main journey through Pandora and includes Main Missions, Side Missions, and Star Challenge replay content for ongoing rewards.
borderlandsMobileCampaignGuide.intro: This guide covers how to enter Borderlands Mobile Campaign, what Chapters unlock, how Main and Side Missions differ, how Star Challenge works, and common mission troubleshooting.

items.question: How do I enter the Campaign feature in Borderlands Mobile?
items.answer: Tap the Travel button, then choose Campaign, which appears first in the selection list.

items.question: What are Chapters in Borderlands Mobile?
items.answer: Chapters guide your journey through Pandora, provide additional challenges, and reward progress with new features, achievements, and more challenging follow-up chapters.

items.question: What is a Main Mission in Borderlands Mobile?
items.answer: Main Missions are missions focused on the current Chapter's storyline and form the core progression path through Borderlands Mobile Campaign.

items.question: What is a Side Mission in Borderlands Mobile?
items.answer: Side Missions unlock as you progress through a Chapter and offer optional objectives with extra rewards beyond the main story.

items.question: How does Star Challenge work in Borderlands Mobile?
items.answer: After completing several chapters, Star Challenge unlocks and lets you replay completed missions. Each replayed mission has three additional challenges, and each completed challenge awards one Star.

items.question: Do all replayed missions in Borderlands Mobile have Star Challenges?
items.answer: Completed missions that are replayed will have Star Challenges except for the Prologue.

items.question: What are Stars used for in Borderlands Mobile Campaign?
items.answer: Earn enough Stars to claim Claptrap's chest of booty from the Star Challenge reward track.

items.question: Why can't I complete the It's Just Business Pt 2 mission in Borderlands Mobile?
items.answer: Jeffrey from Accounting can occasionally fail to spawn in this Side Mission. Closing and reloading the game resolves the issue.

borderlandsMobileVaughnsAdventureGuide.title: Borderlands Mobile Vaughn's Adventure Guide
borderlandsMobileVaughnsAdventureGuide.subtitle: Vaughn's Adventure is a timed reward feature in Borderlands Mobile that pays out XP, Cash, and a Legendary Chest, with rewards scaling as your account levels up.
borderlandsMobileVaughnsAdventureGuide.intro: This guide explains when Vaughn's Adventure unlocks, where to find it, how often to check in, what the two reward timers are, and how Ship upgrades improve your payouts.

items.title: Unlock Requirement
items.text: You unlock Vaughn's Adventure in Borderlands Mobile after rescuing Vaughn during the Friends Like These mission.

items.title: Regular Reward Timer
items.text: Vaughn ventures outside the ship every 10 minutes to collect extra XP and Cash. The timer resets only after you collect the rewards.

items.title: Legendary Chest Timer
items.text: Return after 18 hours to collect the Legendary Chest from Vaughn's Adventure. This timer also resets only after you collect it.

items.title: Where to Access It
items.text: Access Vaughn's Adventure using its designated button on the top-right side of the Borderlands Mobile main screen.

items.title: Level Scaling Rewards
items.text: The amount of Cash and XP you collect from Vaughn's Adventure increases as your Vault Hunter level goes up in Borderlands Mobile.

items.title: Ship Upgrade Synergy
items.text: Upgrading Scavenger Hunt to level 2 adds Parts and Fillers to the items Vaughn finds, expanding the value of each Borderlands Mobile check-in.

borderlandsMobileMarcusMunitionsGuide.title: Borderlands Mobile Marcus Munitions Guide
borderlandsMobileMarcusMunitionsGuide.subtitle: Marcus Munitions is the Borderlands Mobile in-game shop featuring multiple chest tiers, free claim timing, and Ship upgrades that improve what you can earn.
borderlandsMobileMarcusMunitionsGuide.intro: Use this table as your fast reference for chest types, key costs, guaranteed reward rarity, Free Chest timing, and how Ship and Legendary Collection progression connect to the Borderlands Mobile shop.

rows.name: Free Chest
rows.note: Claim once every 10 minutes. Timer resets only after collection.

rows.name: Golden Chest
rows.note: Key-based chest available in Marcus Munitions.

rows.name: Legendary Chest
rows.note: Top-tier chest in Borderlands Mobile Marcus Munitions.

rows.name: Marcus Munitions Level 2
rows.reward: Adds Rare Weapons to Free Chest
rows.note: Upgrade Marcus Munitions to level 2 from the Ship to improve the Free Chest loop.

rows.name: Legendary Collection
rows.note: Shows how and where to get different pieces of Legendary gear in Borderlands Mobile.

borderlandsMobileTowerOfTerrorGuide.title: Borderlands Mobile Tower of Terror Guide
borderlandsMobileTowerOfTerrorGuide.subtitle: Push through enemy floors, spend tickets wisely, and turn repeat clears into Ship Parts. This mode is built for steady ship progression and daily ranking rewards.
borderlandsMobileTowerOfTerrorGuide.intro: Tower of Terror is a single-player challenge mode found from the Travel button on the HQ screen. You clear every enemy on a floor to move forward, spend one Ticket per entry, and can replay completed floors for standard rewards or use Quick Play when you need a fast farm run.

items.title: How to Access the Mode
items.summary: Open the HQ screen, tap Travel, and choose Tower of Terror from the list of game modes.
bullets: Tower of Terror appears in the Travel menu on the HQ screen.
It is a single-player mode.
You advance by killing all enemies on the current floor.

items.title: Tickets and Floor Entry
items.summary: Every attempt costs a Ticket, including repeat clears and Quick Play runs.
bullets: Each floor requires a Ticket to enter.
Replaying a completed floor also consumes a Ticket.
Quick Play can be used on previously completed floors for instant standard rewards.

items.title: Rewards and Ship Parts
items.summary: The mode is one of the main places to grind Ship Parts and first-clear milestone rewards.
bullets: The mode is recommended for farming Ship Parts.
Certain floors grant special rewards from Tina on first completion.
Quick Play grants standard rewards only, not first-clear special rewards.

items.title: Leaderboard and Daily Reset
items.summary: Your ranking updates as you climb, and daily leaderboard rewards are delivered the next day.
bullets: Leaderboards show your standing, the top players, and the highest floors reached.
Your current standing also appears on the floor summary screen.
The leaderboard resets daily.
Top-ranked Vault Hunters receive rewards after logging in the next day.

items.title: What Players on This Floor Means
items.summary: That floor count is informational only and does not create multiplayer combat inside the tower.
bullets: The player count only shows that other players reached the same floor.
You do not play with or against those players inside the floor.

borderlandsMobileCircleOfSlaughterGuide.title: Borderlands Mobile Circle of Slaughter Guide
borderlandsMobileCircleOfSlaughterGuide.subtitle: This survival mode opens after its timer ends and lets you fight enemy waves for repeatable loot runs. It is one of the cleanest ways to target Gear and Mods without paying an entry fee.
borderlandsMobileCircleOfSlaughterGuide.intro: Circle of Slaughter is a challenge mode placed in Mode Selection right next to Tower of Terror. Once the event unlocks, you can enter freely and survive as many waves as possible to improve your reward chances.

items.title: How the Mode Unlocks
items.summary: The event becomes available after its countdown finishes.
bullets: Circle of Slaughter unlocks after the timer ends.
You can find it in Mode Selection next to Tower of Terror.

items.title: How the Mode Works
items.summary: The goal is simple: survive wave after wave for as long as possible.
bullets: You fight waves of enemies in a survival challenge.
Longer survival improves your reward chances.

items.title: Current Stage Count
items.summary: At the moment, the mode has a limited set of playable levels.
bullets: There are currently 2 levels in Circle of Slaughter.
More levels are planned to be added later.

items.title: Rewards and Farming Value
items.summary: The mode is built for repeat runs because entry is free and reward chances scale with survival.
bullets: There is no entrance fee.
You can replay the mode as many times as you want.
Surviving more waves gives higher chances to win Gear and Mods.

items.title: If the Event Does Not Open
items.summary: Close the game and reload to resolve the timer-expiry unlock issue.
bullets: If the event does not unlock when the timer expires, close the game.
Reloading the game resolves the unlock issue.

borderlandsMobileShipUpgradeGuide.title: Borderlands Mobile Ship Upgrade Guide
borderlandsMobileShipUpgradeGuide.subtitle: Ship upgrades connect several of Borderlands Mobile's long-term systems. Spend Ship Parts to grow the ship, unlock new allies and rooms, and improve what your account earns over time.
borderlandsMobileShipUpgradeGuide.intro: You unlock the Ship feature during the initial playthrough, and it appears on the upper-right side of the screen. Ship Parts are the core upgrade resource, and room upgrades can unlock new modes, improve passive finds, and strengthen shop rewards.

steps.title: Unlock the Ship
steps.details: Progress through the initial playthrough to unlock the Ship feature, then open it from the upper-right side of the screen.

steps.title: Spend Ship Parts
steps.details: Use collected Ship Parts to upgrade the ship, meet new allies, and unlock additional rooms.

steps.title: Use Rooms for Buffs
steps.details: Upgraded rooms provide buffs and increase your ship's overall power.

steps.title: Bridge Level 2 Unlock
steps.details: Upgrading the Bridge to level 2 unlocks Tiny Tina's Tower of Terror.

steps.title: Scavenger Hunt Level 2 Upgrade
steps.details: Upgrading Scavenger Hunt to level 2 adds Parts and Fillers to the items that Vaughn finds for you.

steps.title: Marcus Munitions Level 2 Upgrade
steps.details: Upgrading Marcus Munitions to level 2 adds Rare Weapons to the store's Free Chest.

borderlandsMobileBattlePassGuide.title: Borderlands Mobile Battle Pass Guide
borderlandsMobileBattlePassGuide.subtitle: The Battle Pass is a seasonal progression layer with separate free and premium reward tracks. Your progress is driven by Daily and Weekly Challenges, so routine task completion is the key to climbing tiers.
borderlandsMobileBattlePassGuide.intro: Open the Battle Pass from the button on the left side of the HQ screen. Each season has a time-limited rewards track, and Battle Pass Points come from challenge completion rather than passive login alone.

phases.title: Open the Battle Pass
phases.details: The Battle Pass is accessed from the Battle Pass button on the left side of the HQ screen.

phases.title: Follow the Two Reward Tracks
phases.details: Each season includes two reward tiers: Free Pass and Ultimate Pass.

phases.title: Earn Battle Pass Points
phases.details: Battle Pass Points are earned by completing Daily Challenges and Weekly Challenges, which increase your Battle Pass level and move you up the rewards track.

phases.title: Use the Task Loop Efficiently
phases.details: Daily Tasks must be completed within 24 hours. Weekly Tasks run within the current week, unlock new challenges each day, and have their own rewards.

phases.title: Understand the Ultimate Pass
phases.details: Ultimate Pass unlocks the premium rewards track through in-app purchase and grants additional rewards that match your current Battle Pass progress.

phases.title: Keep Leveling After Purchase
phases.details: Buying the Ultimate Pass does not finish the track automatically. You still need to earn more Battle Pass Points to unlock higher tiers on both reward tracks.

faq.title: Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: Common questions about Borderlands Mobile for new Vault Hunters

questions.question: Is Borderlands Mobile free to play?
questions.answer: Yes. Borderlands Mobile is a free download on the App Store with no upfront purchase required.

questions.question: Which devices support Borderlands Mobile?
questions.answer: Borderlands Mobile is currently available on iPhone only. iPhone 11 or newer is recommended for the best experience. The game is not optimized for tablets or iPads.

questions.question: Is Borderlands Mobile available on Android?
questions.answer: Android support has not been announced yet. The current release is limited to iPhone as part of a limited-time test.

questions.question: How large is the Borderlands Mobile download?
questions.answer: Borderlands Mobile requires approximately 2.9 GB of storage. Make sure you have enough space before downloading from the App Store.

questions.question: What iOS version does Borderlands Mobile require?
questions.answer: Borderlands Mobile requires iOS 18.0 or later. Update your iPhone to the latest iOS version for the best compatibility.

questions.question: How do I level up in Borderlands Mobile?
questions.answer: Your Vault Hunter gains XP by completing missions and defeating enemies in Campaign mode. Each level permanently increases your Power Score and Max HP.

cta.description: Join the Borderlands community and download Borderlands Mobile on iPhone to start your Vault Hunter journey!

beginner.title: Borderlands Mobile Beginner Guide
beginner.description: First-session tips, Campaign progression, XP farming, and Power Score basics for new Vault Hunters.
beginner.metaTitle: Borderlands Mobile Beginner Guide - First Steps and Campaign Tips
beginner.metaDescription: Borderlands Mobile beginner guide covering Campaign mode, how to earn XP, Power Score explained, and Star Challenges for new Vault Hunters.
beginner.whatIsDescription: A first-session walkthrough covering Campaign mode, XP progression, Power Score tracking, and early efficiency tips.

whySteps.step1Description: Learn which missions to prioritize in your first session to avoid early dead ends.
whySteps.step2Description: Understand which actions increase Power Score fastest for smoother mission progression.
whySteps.step3Description: Find out how to access Star Challenges and replay cleared missions for extra rewards.

vault-hunters.title: Borderlands Mobile Vault Hunters
vault-hunters.description: Playable class overview, Summoner abilities, skill system, and future class expansion plans.
vault-hunters.metaTitle: Borderlands Mobile Vault Hunters - Summoner Class Guide and Skills
vault-hunters.metaDescription: Borderlands Mobile Vault Hunter class guide covering the Summoner, Good Boy! Action Skill, skill points, and future class expansion.
vault-hunters.whatIsDescription: An overview of the playable Vault Hunter classes in Borderlands Mobile, starting with the Summoner and its phase-energy beast summoning.

whySteps.step1Description: Learn the Summoner identity, Action Skill, and how it fits the current build.
whySteps.step2Description: Know when skills unlock at level 8 and how to spend points efficiently.
whySteps.step3Description: Learn what additional classes and options are planned for future updates.

weapons.title: Borderlands Mobile Weapons
weapons.description: Weapon types, rarity tiers, legendary loot, and how to upgrade your gear for stronger combat.
weapons.metaTitle: Borderlands Mobile Weapons - Rarity Guide and Legendary Loot
weapons.metaDescription: Borderlands Mobile weapons guide covering gun types, five rarity tiers, legendary loot farming, and upgrade paths for Vault Hunters.
weapons.whatIsDescription: A complete breakdown of weapon types, rarity tiers, and how to find and upgrade the best gear in Borderlands Mobile.

whySteps.step1Description: Understand the five rarity levels and how they affect weapon performance.
whySteps.step2Description: Learn which activities drop the highest-rarity weapons most efficiently.
whySteps.step3Description: Discover how to rank up weapons, grenades, and shields to increase Power Score.

missions.title: Borderlands Mobile Missions
missions.description: Campaign chapter guide, Main and Side Mission breakdown, and strategies for completing story objectives.
missions.metaTitle: Borderlands Mobile Missions - Campaign Guide and Chapter Walkthrough
missions.metaDescription: Borderlands Mobile missions guide covering Campaign chapters, Main Mission order, Side Mission rewards, and Star Challenge tips.
missions.whatIsDescription: A chapter-by-chapter Campaign guide explaining mission types, objectives, and the best order to complete them.

whySteps.step1Description: Learn the difference between Main Missions, Side Missions, and Star Challenges.
whySteps.step2Description: Know which missions to prioritize for the fastest Campaign advancement.
whySteps.step3Description: Find out how to access bonus challenges on completed missions for extra loot.

modes.title: Borderlands Mobile Modes
modes.description: Tower of Terror, Circle of Slaughter, and other gameplay modes beyond the main Campaign.
modes.metaTitle: Borderlands Mobile Modes - Tower of Terror and Circle of Slaughter Guide
modes.metaDescription: Borderlands Mobile modes guide covering Tower of Terror, Circle of Slaughter rules, rewards, and tips for getting the most out of each mode.
modes.whatIsDescription: An overview of Borderlands Mobile's repeatable game modes including Tower of Terror and Circle of Slaughter.

whySteps.step1Description: Learn how Tower of Terror works and which rewards are worth farming.
whySteps.step2Description: Understand the wave-based combat format and how to survive longer for better loot.
whySteps.step3Title: Pick the right mode for your goal
whySteps.step3Description: Know which mode gives XP, gear upgrades, or mod drops most efficiently.
